name: Ammar ibn Abi Salama
title: Soldier of Insight
shortDescription: A loyal Shia and soldier of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) who rushed to the Master of Martyrs' aid and attained his wish of martyrdom on Ashura.
fullStory:
Ammar ibn Abi Salama al-Dalani was a sincere Shia and brave warrior of Kufa. During the caliphate of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS), he was considered a close companion and had wielded his sword alongside his master in the battles of Siffin, Jamal, and Nahrawan.

When Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad came to Kufa and made conditions harsh for Shias, Ammar tried to assassinate Ibn Ziyad on the road but was unsuccessful. He then left Kufa and reached Karbala before the war started to be by Imam Hussain's (AS) side.

Ammar, who had vast experience from the early Islamic battles, put on battle armor with an epic spirit on the day of Ashura. Having spent his life in jihad, he went to the battlefield this time to defend the most oppressed Imam of his time.

In the all-out attack on the morning of Ashura (the first attack), Ammar stood alongside other warriors and resisted the rain of arrows and the cowardly attack of the Kufans with indescribable bravery.

Finally, after a fierce battle, this valiant elder of Wilayah rolled in his own blood, and his lofty soul joined his martyred companions from the early Islamic battles.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى عَمّارِ بْنِ أَبي سَلامَةَ الدّالانِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Ammar ibn Abi Salama al-Dalani.
